text.div: intentionally left empty / not meaningful

tr.td: Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
25 micrograms/hour transdermal patch applied every 72 hours
Amlodipine (as amlodipine besylate) 2.5 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (2.5 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (50 mg) every 6 hours as needed
Acetaminophen 300 mg and hydrocodone bitartrate 5 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (300 mg/5 mg) by mouth every 4 to 6 hours as needed for pain
Metformin hydrochloride 500 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
Alendronic acid (as alendronate sodium) 10 mg oral tablet
Oxycodone hydrochloride 10 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
Galantamine (as galantamine hydrobromide) 4 mg oral tablet
Clopidogrel (as clopidogrel bisulfate) 75 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily
1 spray (400 micrograms) under the tongue as needed for chest pain, may repeat every 5 minutes as needed, up to 3 sprays in 15 minutes
Metoprolol tartrate (as metoprolol succinate) 100 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
Vitamin B12 and/or vitamin B12 derivative-containing product
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Assessment of health and social care needs
Referral to home health care service
Urinalysis with reflex to microscopy and culture
Assessment using AUDIT-C (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test - Consumption)

ul.li: Maintain current level of cognitive function and slow progression of cognitive decline associated with Alzheimer's disease
Support the patient to maintain independence in basic activities of daily living for as long as possible
Reduce frequency and severity of behavioral and psychological symptoms of dementia
Ensure consistent adherence to prescribed dementia medications to maximize therapeutic benefit
Provide resources and support to caregivers to reduce caregiver burden and improve quality of life
Maintain HbA1c at or below individualized target to optimize glycemic control and reduce complications.
Maintain blood pressure below 140/90 mmHg to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Maintain LDL cholesterol below 70 mg/dL to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Maintain fasting blood glucose between 80 and 130 mg/dL to optimize glycemic control.
Maintain triglycerides below 150 mg/dL to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Achieve and maintain target blood pressure through lifestyle modifications.

tr.td: Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Blood pressure panel with all children optional
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

text.value: Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Support the patient to maintain independence in basic activities of daily living for as long as possible
Reduce frequency and severity of behavioral and psychological symptoms of dementia
Ensure consistent adherence to prescribed dementia medications to maximize therapeutic benefit

Maintain HbA1c at or below individualized target to optimize glycemic control and reduce complications.

Maintain LDL cholesterol below 70 mg/dL to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Maintain fasting blood glucose between 80 and 130 mg/dL to optimize glycemic control.
Maintain triglycerides below 150 mg/dL to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Achieve and maintain target blood pressure through lifestyle modifications.
